reviews.text: We booked a pricier 1-bedroom suite that seemed designed by a novice.  With the separating wall, it was very dark.  The room seemed old and the vanity marble had been damaged and about 1/2 inch pushed down on one side.  We asked for an upgrade and were lucky to get a better room with nice view of the airport runways (actually liked that as it wasn't too loud) but connected to other rooms that were really in need of soundproofing the doors.  No safe for passports and valuables, otherwise not bad but had to pay for the upgrade.  I think regular room with airport view likely better than any upgrades available here.  Not sure though.

reviews.title: Bad shuttle service and 15 USD wifi
reviews.positives: Pool area is nice. It's clean and conveniently located
reviews.negatives:
Waited 45 minutes for the shuttle. When it arrived, a bunch of businessmen cut the line,  leaving me and my 4yo kid waiting for the next one. Driver, who didn't speak a word of English, assured me through Google translate that the next shuttle was 5 minutes out. 15 minutes later, I called the hotel (the phone number on booking.com is incorrect btw), they told me the next shuttle will arrive momentarily. Another 15 minutes later it did. Front desk showed little interest in my story but assured me she'll forward the concern to the shuttle company. You'd really expect better from an airport hotel and not outsource the shuttle. You know, if anyone had told me that the shuttle takes so long, I'd have taken an Uber, no problem. But instead it was just shit communication.
Another disgusting move is that they'll charge you 15 USD for WiFi, which probably really sucks if you arrive as a foreigner actually needing WiFi. What hotel doesn't have free WiFi in this day and age??

reviews.title: Scam
reviews.text: We stayed  for a number of nights paid in full via Agoda.  Our credit card registered for deposit was pinging my phone for charges throughout our stay even though we only ate once in the restaurant and had no further charges.  Returning back home, I looked at my credit card statement and there are numerous charges.  I had complained during my stay and had two of the front desk personnel assure me that there were no mistakes, even have some calculations on my checkout bill.  None of it happened and I have to call their 'corporate' office to resolve.  What a pain!  The room was full paid and a significant deposit was taken.  It should have been one deposit, deduct the small charges, and receive the difference.  They seemed as confused as I was at explaining fees and now I know it wasn't just me.  Never had such a convoluted explanation and they finished with 'just call if there's a problem'.  100s of business trips and this was an obvious mismanagement or scam, either way, such wasted time making sure I get reimbursed and my business doesn't think I'm trying to charge them for unrealized expenses. Very unhappy about that.

reviews.title: Horrible, Unacceptable and Never Again.
reviews.positives: I booked two rooms with Booking.com. Rooms prepaid and non-cancellable. Late check-in confirmed (9pm) as well as room reservations by email a week before arrival. One reservation, two rooms, under two guest names. After 30 hours of travel, 3 flights, upon check-in, my room was registered and key given, the reservation for my friend, room #2 could not be found. The staff at registration was not helpful, they didn't look at the reservation carefully, said I had to sort it out with Booking.com. No manager was available. No staff wanted to assist. It took over 90 minutes to sort. My 80 YO physically challenged companion sat and waited 90 minutes for his prepaid room at 10PM at night after a grueling 30 hour travel day. I ended up getting Booking on the line, carried the phone to the desk and FORCED a staff to talk to Booking.com. Westin had instructed them (through me) to call the Westin, which Booking did on multiple tries to multiple numbers with no Westin staff answering the phone or the e-mails Booking sent. No contact responded, my friend sat locked out. I then carried the phone down to the lobby from my room with the Booking rep on the line and forced them to resolve it. After the call, after looking at the reservation the 3rd time, a different staff said "oh, there it is" and saw the second room on the original Booking Reservation provided earlier. It was there the whole time. The Reservation System computers had taken his middle initial and moved it as the first letter of his last name. Still, no excuse for their lack of effort and ability to sort a prepaid reservation for two rooms. No apologies, only blame, excuse making and whining. VERY unprofessional! Never again. We checked out at 7AM next day with little to no sleep. Booking.com was helpful, Westin was not.
reviews.negatives: Registration/Check-In staff, training, effort, attitude, lack of language skills, lack of problem solving skills, lack of Hospitality Industry professionalism and no management on duty or willing to come out of their office.

reviews.title: great hotel for fliers needing a layover
reviews.text: We stayed for one night before a long-haul flight. Check-in was quick and efficient. The room was spacious, and if there was a little scuffing on some surfaces, it didn't detract from the overall comfort of the room.  Our room was quite quiet despite the proximity to the airport.  Unfortunately, our view was unattractive (mainly over a drainage ditch and a roof), but airport hotels are mainly for sleeping anyway.  Location is very good:  there's an In & Out Burger just over the overpass (about a 10-min. walk) - with a BART station just across the road - and a couple of grocery stores about a 20-min. walk away.  The free wifi in the lobby is nice, though obviously, it would be great if it were available in the rooms.  The staff is helpful about storing luggage and answering questions.  The free shuttle has an excellent schedule.  If we have cause to need an SFO airport hotel, we would choose The Westin again.

reviews.title: Westin sacrifices their profit by staying open for their guests. They have won my respect & loyalty!
reviews.text:
When the going gets tough, only the tough gets going - it surely depicts the flighters like Westin hotel.

With the COVID-19 situation worsening, many flights are cancelled and hotels closed, leaving the travellers desperate. For the whole week prior to my date of travel, I was extremely nervous that I will have no place to stay between flights. Then I received a letter from the hotel group CEO, stating that Westin will stay open. 

By the time I arrived, it saddens me to see the empty hotel with closed restaurants and single-digit guests. I realize how hard it is for a hotel to stay open at a time like this - thinking about the daily costs with barely any income stream! It shows the nobility of a hotel - they do put their guests in front of their own profits. For that, I solute to Westin - they have won my respect and loyalty! 

Westin is like a trustworthy old friend - they might not be the coolest, but they are the kind that will support you when you need it. I love them!

Staff that worked at restaurant are very friendly, hardworking and engaging. Like the shuttle bus service and the bus driver was super friendly and helpful with our luggages when we arrived but we have a different bus driver the next day and he was okay.
reviews.negatives: Check-out is slow. Only 1 staff and chatting away with small talks with the guest checking out causing a delay and there were other guests coming up too. Be professional and respect that there is a line and not everyone likes to hear small talk when we are in a hurry and wants to get checked out to make it in time for the shuttle. Other than that, everything was wonderful!

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Airport shuttle was wonderful and the driver was very helpful
reviews.negatives: When I arrived I needed help with my suitcases and someone to help me take them up to the room. I had two big suitcases and a carry on. I have a back condition and I wear a brace on my wrist for an injury. I asked for help and they said that they do not have someone to take the suitcases up. I had to struggle with my luggage to the elevator, in and out of the elevator, then drag them one at a time to the door of the room. When I had everything in front of the room, I unlocked and dragged them one at a time inside. I did the same while leaving, to find that a guest had all their luggage in a wheeled cart and was transporting their luggage easily. Front desk did not offer that option and watched me drag my luggage. I expected better from a Mariott.

Parking very expensive at $48 per day, staff were professional however very uninviting. I shared it was my birthday and zero care. Wasn’t told how to use the elevator as you need to swipe a room key so we spent 10 minutes confused until another guest assisted us (no signs). We helped several others during our stay so it’s a common thing. Also wasn’t told about the amenity coupons included with the destination fee. I overheard it on my 3rd day and luckily we were able to retrieve them. 
 Free drink was a house Chardonnay that I couldn’t drink as it tasted like an old opened bottle. Tried the pork belly sliders which were entirely fat. The spring rolls were good. 

The housekeeping was missed day 1 and day 2 was not cleaned well. Food and sticky surfaces unwiped etc. need vent cleaning badly as the whole property has caked up debris throughout. Pool lounge wear worn and looked uncleaned and hot tub smelled strongly of bleach which you know what that means 😬.

Pot isn’t the most pleasant smell. I would recommend having someon walk the floors and identify culprits. There are “No smoking “ signs around the hotel. Other than that, nice convenient place to stay
